title: Creamy Egyptian Claypot Sake Dish
description: A fresh, creamy, and quick-fix Egyptian dish featuring sake, clay-pot-cooked to perfection, and suitable for a macrobiotic-friendly diet.
instructions: Rinse a ceramic tagine or clay pot, and set it aside for use.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the tagine over medium heat.
Add 1 small onion, finely chopped, and sauté until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add 2 cloves of garlic, minced, and sauté for another minute.
Stir in 1 cup of sake, and bring to a simmer.
Add 2 cups of mixed vegetables, such as carrots, zucchini, and bell peppers, and cook until they're tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in 1/2 cup of plain yogurt, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the tagine to the preheated oven, and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened slightly.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley, and serve hot, over rice or with pita bread.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving, to allow the flavors to settle.
Serve warm, garnished with additional parsley, if desired.
